tools.name: check_logic
tools.description: Catch contradictions in reasoning before acting on it. FREE — no account needed. Extracts quantitative and logical claims from any plan, calculation, or chain of thought, then uses a Z3 SAT solver to mathematically prove whether they contradict each other. This is formal verification, not an LLM sec

tools.name: check_relevance
tools.description: Free relevance screen — checks whether an action touches any of your policy variables before running a paid check. Returns a relevance score and which variables matched. If should_check is true, run check_action. If false, the action is unrelated to your policy — skip the paid check. No credits char

tools.name: make_rules
tools.description: Turn a plain-English policy into a jailbreak-proof guardrail. Write rules the way you would explain them to a colleague — ICME compiles them into formal logic (SMT-LIB) that a mathematical solver enforces. No prompt engineering. No LLM judges. The solver either proves an action complies or it doesn'

tools.name: check_action
tools.description: Enforce a guardrail: verify an agent action against a compiled policy using formal verification. An SMT solver — not an LLM — determines whether the action satisfies every rule. Returns SAT (allowed) or UNSAT (blocked) with extracted values and a cryptographic ZK proof that the check was performed c

tools.name: check_action_paid
tools.description: Same formal verification as check_action, but pay per call with x402 ($0.10 USDC on Base) instead of using credits. No API key or account needed — any agent with a wallet can verify actions on the fly. Returns SAT (allowed) or UNSAT (blocked) with extracted values and optional ZK proof.

tools.name: quick_check
tools.description: Fast yes/no guardrail verdict — returns only ALLOWED or BLOCKED with no proof details. Use when you need a lightweight gate and don't need the full SAT/UNSAT report or ZK proof. Same formal verification under the hood, just a minimal response. 1 credit. Requires api_key.

tools.name: verify_proof
tools.description: Independently verify a ZK proof from a prior check_action call. Confirms the guardrail check was performed correctly without re-running it — any third party or monitoring agent can verify in under one second. No additional cost. Wait a few minutes after the check for the proof to be generated. Singl

tools.name: get_scenarios
tools.description: Review auto-generated test scenarios for a compiled policy. Shows example actions that should pass and fail, so you can verify the policy behaves as intended before deploying it. Requires api_key.

tools.name: run_tests
tools.description: Run saved test cases against a policy to confirm it blocks what it should block and allows what it should allow. Run this after make_rules and before using the policy in production. Requires api_key.

tools.name: create_account
tools.description: Create an ICME Preflight account with x402 USDC payment ($5.00 on Base). Returns an API key and 325 starting credits immediately. Save the api_key — it is shown only once. Use create_account_card instead if paying by credit card.

tools.name: create_account_card
tools.description: Create an ICME Preflight account with a credit card ($5.00 via Stripe). Returns a checkout_url — open it in a browser to pay. After payment completes, call poll_session with the session_id to retrieve your API key.

tools.name: top_up
tools.description: Add 500 credits to your account via x402 USDC payment ($5.00 on Base). Use top_up_card instead for credit card payment with volume discounts. Requires api_key.

tools.name: top_up_card
tools.description: Add credits via credit card with volume bonuses. Returns a checkout_url. Tiers: $5 = 500 credits, $10 = 1,050 (+5%), $25 = 2,750 (+10%), $50 = 5,750 (+15%), $100 = 12,000 (+20%). Credits never expire. Requires api_key.

tools.name: poll_session
tools.description: Check the status of a Stripe card payment. Returns pending or complete. On completion after signup, returns the api_key. Call this after create_account_card or top_up_card once the user has paid.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "check_relevance" references sensitive artifacts (api_key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "run_tests" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Run saved test cases against a policy to confirm it blocks what it should block and allows what it should allow. Run this after make_rules and before using the …

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.
